one_line_description: Web hosting and deployment platform for static sites and serverless functions with build automation and edge services.
auth_summary: The Netlify API (open-API/REST) accepts personal access tokens generated in user settings, sent as 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'. Public/third-party integrations must use OAuth2 (authorize endpoint at app.netlify.com/authorize).
access_notes: Free-tier Netlify accounts can create personal access tokens under Applications > Personal access tokens instantly; OAuth apps are also self-registered.
api_surface_summary: REST API covers sites, deploys, builds, DNS/domains, environment variables, forms, and account/team management — the full hosting control plane, though narrower in scope than hyperscaler APIs.
agent_notes: Official MCP (@netlify/mcp) wraps both API and CLI capabilities (deploys, env vars, extensions). API is described by an OpenAPI spec, which makes toolkit generation easy.
